title: DERELICT
byline: Mining vessel 'Kestrel-9'. You don't remember going under.

character.backstory: Twelve years crewing deep-belt ore haulers. You went under in cryo with ten others and woke alone. You know this ship's reactor and hull better than your own hands - which is why the things you cannot explain frighten you so much.

watching: IT WANTS YOU AWAKE WHEN IT-

cryobay.title: Cryo-bay C
cryobay.text:
Cold. The kind that has teeth. You come apart from sleep one nerve at a time, lid of the cryopod hissing back over your face. The bay is dark except for a single amber fault-light, ticking on, off, on.

Eleven other pods line the wall. Ten of them are open. Empty. The readout above yours blinks a date you don't trust.

choices.text: Stay still. Listen to the ship first.

listen.text:
You hold your breath and let the ship speak. The hull ticks as it cools. Then, far down the spine, a drag - long, unhurried - that stops the instant you go still, as if it had been listening for exactly that.

You decide not to listen anymore.

cryobay_up.title: Cryo-bay C
cryobay_up.text: Your legs hold. An emergency locker is set into the wall, its seal cracked. Beyond the bay, the corridor runs fore and aft.

locker_done.title: Cryo-bay C
locker_done.text: A hand torch, still charged. A single med-gel ampoule beside it - the field kind, for crew who stop trusting what they see. You take both. The light feels like a small, stupid kind of courage.

corridor.title: Spinal corridor
corridor.text: You follow the spine of the ship, dust hanging in the still air. Three ways lead off it: the bridge fore, the med-bay to port, engineering aft. And straight ahead, a stretch where no light reaches - a black gap that gives nothing back.

sanityText.40: The corridor breathes. You know corridors don't, you know it, but the walls swell and settle and the dust hangs like it's deciding something. Three doorways. A black gap ahead that is looking back.

choices.text: Push into the dark stretch ahead.

bridge.title: Bridge
bridge.text: The viewport is full of stars that aren't any you know. The captain's chair is turned to face the door, empty. The log terminal still has power; the last entry is timestamped, then nothing.

bridge_log.title: Bridge
bridge_log.text: The captain's voice is calm in the way of someone who has already given up. "We woke it in the ore. It doesn't want the ship. It wants us awake when it - " The recording ends in a sound you will hear again behind your eyes for as long as you have eyes.

medbay.title: Med-bay
medbay.text: The med-bay smells of antiseptic and something underneath it. Two crew lie on the slabs under sheets that were white once. A supply cabinet hangs ajar.

choices.text: Lift the sheet on the nearest slab.

medbay_cab.title: Med-bay
medbay_cab.text: One med-gel ampoule rolls to the back of the cabinet. You pocket it. Small mercy.

choices.text: Lift the sheet on the nearest slab.

medbay_sheet.title: Med-bay
medbay_sheet.text: You shouldn't have. The body is intact and entirely wrong - the face fixed mid-expression, not pain, not fear. Attention. Whatever happened, this one was paying attention right to the end. You let the sheet fall.

engineering.title: Engineering
engineering.text: The reactor takes up the whole aft bulkhead, a black slab, the ship's one heart. Without it: no heat, no scrubbers, no voice in the walls. A toolbox lies spilled across the deck plating.

sanityText.30: A hum in here you feel in your fillings, pitched below hearing. The toolbox has spilled, and the tools that remain point - every one of them - toward the dark stretch of corridor.

choices.text: Take the hydrospanner from the toolbox.

choices.locked: Open the reactor access panel. (bolted shut - you need the hydrospanner from the toolbox)

engineering_power.title: Engineering
engineering_power.text: The panel comes free. You re-seat the breakers and the reactor catches with a deep, resentful hum. Down the corridor, lights stutter to life one bank at a time - racing toward the dark stretch, then dying again exactly where it begins.

darkgap.title: The dark stretch
darkgap.text: The dark here is total. It does not thin as your eyes adjust. Air moves against your face - exhaled, not vented. Something is here with you, between you and the escape bay beyond.

choices.text: Raise the torch and look directly at it.
Kill the torch and feel along the wall in the black.

darkgap_lit.title: The dark stretch
darkgap_lit.text: You raise the beam. It's the captain - or it's wearing the captain - folded into the ceiling space, head tilted, paying a fixed, total attention. It does not move toward you. It simply lets you see. A keycard hangs from the corpse's belt; you take it with hands that won't stop shaking, and it lets you.

darkgap_blind.title: The dark stretch
darkgap_blind.text: You kill the light and trust your hands. Halfway through, your fingers find warmth on the wall at chest height - the give of a face, and the slow turn of it following your palm as you pass. You do not stop. You do not breathe. Something is missing from you when you reach the far side, and you don't know yet what it was.

escape.title: Escape bay
escape.text: One pod remains in its cradle, intact, waiting. Its console asks for two things: command authorisation, and power.

choices.text: Insert the captain's keycard and launch.
choices.locked: Insert authorisation and launch. (nothing launches without the captain's keycard and a live reactor)

choices.text: Forget the pod. Open the outer airlock and walk out into the stars.

ending.text:
The pod tears free of the cradle. Through the port, the Kestrel-9 shrinks to a dead grey splinter against the wrong stars - and on its hull, for just a moment, something turns to watch you go. Then distance takes it.

You are awake. You are alone. You are alive. Two of those will fade with time.

There is not enough of you left to want the pod. Every wrong thing you made yourself look at took a piece, and this is what is left - a mind rubbed thin, and grateful now for any door that opens.

The outer door cycles. The air leaves in one long breath and you step out smiling. Behind you, the thing that wanted you awake gets the last of what it came for. The ship goes dark.
